behavior: Usually moves alone or in pairs through low, tangled cover, hopping, creeping, and probing bark, leaf litter, and crevices for invertebrates; often holds the tail cocked while foraging and gives loud, repeated whistled songs.
field_marks: Small, compact wren with a round body and large-headed look
Warm buffy-orange underparts with a clean white throat and chin
Long, bold white eyebrow stripe above a dark eye
Long, slender, slightly downcurved dark bill
Long tail, often cocked upward; wings and tail show dark barring
habitat: Dense, brushy cover in open woodland, forest understory and edges, thickets, ravines, overgrown farmland, gardens, and shrubby residential areas.
